notes: GrabBus was a ticket-booking marketplace (2019, beta) for existing P2P bus operators, not an independent transit operator. Service appears discontinued circa 2020 (COVID-19 disruptions). No formal discontinuation announcement found. Current Grab Philippines app offers no bus or shuttle services to the public. Corporate RideCo-powered shuttles are private and not public transit. All routes listed below duplicated in P2P bus operator data.

routes.frequency_notes: Fixed scheduled departures; advance booking required via Grab app
routes.notes: Historical route (2019 launch). Operated by Tas Trans / San Agustin on behalf of GrabBus marketplace. Service likely discontinued ~2020. Duplicated in P2P bus operator data.

routes.notes: Historical route (2019 launch). Operated by Tas Trans / San Agustin. Service likely discontinued ~2020. Out-of-NCR destination (Laguna); only partially within NCR scope. Duplicated in P2P bus operator data.

routes.notes: Added September 2019. Destination is in Cavite, outside NCR. Service likely discontinued ~2020. Partial NCR relevance (Makati origin only).
